params.name: EPR Paradox
params.description: In this paper, the trio, through thought experiments, questioned the completeness of quantum mechanics. The EPR paradox, based on the phenomenon of quantum entanglement, posits that two or more particles are entangled in certain physical properties. Even when separated by great distances, measuring the state of one particle affects the state of the other, with the influence occurring almost instantaneously, violating the causality and speed of light constraints of special relativity. This parado …
